modules.title: Introduction to Automata Theory
topics: Finite Automata as language acceptor and translator
Conversion from Mealy to Moore and vice versa

modules.title: Types of Finite Automata
topics: Language operations: union, intersection, concatenation, closure

modules.title: Grammars
topics: Conversion of grammar to automata and vice versa
Elimination of null and unit productions

modules.title: Pushdown Automata
topics: Conversion of PDA to CFG and vice versa

modules.title: Turing Machines and Decidability
topics: Multi‑tape, multi‑head and multidimensional Turing machines

experiments: Design a program for creating a machine that accepts three consecutive ones.
Design a program for creating a machine that accepts strings always ending with 101.
Design a program for Mode‑3 machine.
Design a program for accepting decimal numbers divisible by 2.
Design a program for creating a machine which accepts strings having equal numbers of 1’s and 0’s.
Design a program for creating a machine which counts the number of 1’s and 0’s in a given string.
Design a program to find the 2’s complement of a given binary number.
Design a program which will increment the given binary number by 1.
Design a program to convert NFA to DFA.
Design a program to create a PDA that accepts well‑formed parentheses.
Design a PDA to accept strings of the form w C wᴿ where w is any string, wᴿ is the reverse of w, and C is a special symbol.
Design a Turing machine that accepts the language aⁿ bⁿ cⁿ where n > 0.
reference_books: Introduction to Automata Theory, Languages & Computation – Hopcroft & Ullman (Narosa Publication).
Elements of the Theory of Computation – Lewis & Christos (Pearson).
Theory of Computation – Chandrasekhar & Mishra (PHI).
Theory of Computation – Wood (Harper & Row).
Introduction to Computing Theory – Daniel I‑A. Cohen (Wiley).

modules.title: Computer Network: Definitions, goals, components, Architecture, Classifications & Types; Layered Architecture; ISO/OSI Reference Model; Physical Layer Principles
topics: Definitions, goals, components, architecture, classifications and types of computer networks.
Protocol hierarchy and layered architecture, including design issues, interfaces and services.
Connection‑oriented and connectionless services and associated service primitives.
ISO/OSI reference model and its comparison with the TCP/IP model.
Physical layer principles: transmission media, bandwidth, data rate and modulation schemes.

modules.title: Data Link Layer
topics: Data link layer functions: services, framing, flow control and error control.
Elementary and sliding‑window protocols (1‑bit, Go‑Back‑N, Selective‑Repeat, Hybrid ARQ).
Protocol verification using finite‑state‑machine and Petri‑net models.

modules.title: MAC Sub‑layer
topics: MAC sub‑layer addressing and the Binary Exponential Back‑off (BEB) algorithm.
Random access and contention schemes (ALOHA, Slotted ALOHA, CSMA, CSMA/CD, CSMA/CA).
Collision‑free protocols (Basic Bit Map, BRAP, Binary Count Down, MLMA, Adaptive Tree Walk).
IEEE 802 series standards and their variants.

modules.title: Network Layer
topics: Network layer services, design issues and routing algorithms (Least‑Cost, Dijkstra, Bellman‑Ford, hierarchical, broadcast, multicast).
IP addressing, header format, packet forwarding, fragmentation and reassembly.
Comparative study of IPv4 and IPv6.

modules.title: Transport and Application Layers
topics: Transport layer design issues, UDP header format, checksum and support for unicast/multicast real‑time traffic.
TCP connection management, reliability mechanisms, flow control, congestion control, header format and timer management.
Application layer protocols: HTTP/WWW, FTP, SSH, SMTP/MIME/IMAP, DNS, and network management (SNMP).

experiments: Study of Different Types of LAN & Network Equipments.
Study and Verification of standard Network topologies i.e. Star, Bus, Ring etc.
LAN installations and Configurations.
Write a program to implement various types of error correcting techniques.
Write a program to implement various types of farming methods.
Study of Tool Command Language (TCL).
Study and Installation of Standard Network Simulators: NS‑2, NS‑3, OpNet, QualNet etc.
Study & Installation of ONE (Opportunistic Network Environment) Simulator for High Mobility Networks.
Configure 802.11 WLAN.
Implement & Simulate various types of routing algorithms.
Study & Simulation of MAC Protocols like Aloha, CSMA, CSMA/CD and CSMA/CA using Standard Network Simulators.
Study of Application layer protocols – DNS, HTTP, HTTPS, FTP and Telnet.
reference_books: Andrew S. Tanenbaum, David J. Wetherall, “Computer Networks”, Pearson Education.
Douglas E. Comer, “Internetworking With TCP/IP: Principles, Protocols, and Architecture – Volume I”, 6th Edition, Pearson Education.
Dimitri Bertsekas, Robert Gallager, “Data Networks”, PHI Publication, Second Edition.
Kaveh Pahlavan, Prashant Krishnamurthy, “Networking Fundamentals”, Wiley Publication.
U. Y. Black, “Computer Networks”, PHI Publication, Second Edition.
Ying-Dar Lin, Ren-Hung Hwang, Fred Baker, “Computer Networks: An Open Source Approach”, McGraw Hill.

modules.title: Module 2
topics: Finding contours and edges of objects

modules.title: Module 3
topics: Tracking a single point over time
Analyzing videos as sequences of frames
Tracking a set of features over time
Tracking a moving car using optical flow

modules.title: Module 4
topics: Histogram filter for robot localization in Python

experiments: Various forms of image representation
Apply various image segmentation algorithms
Apply object motion and tracking
Apply object localization
Apply image restoration
reference_books: Audio Video Systems, Bali & Bali, Khanna Book Publishing 2020.
Handbook of Image and Video Processing by Alan C. Bovik, Academic Press, 2000.
Python 3 Image Processing, Ashwin Pajankar, BPB Publication, 2019.
https://www.coursera.org/learn/image-processing

topics: Overview of the data analyst ecosystem
Big data processing tools (Hadoop, HDFS, Hive, Spark)

Industry project examples – creating visualizations of complex datasets

Annalyn Ng, Kenneth Soo, *Numsense! Data Science for the Layman*, Shroff Publisher.
Cathy O’Neil and Rachel Schutt, *Doing Data Science*, O’Reilly Media.
Jure Leskovec, Anand Rajaraman and Jeffrey Ullman, *Mining of Massive Datasets*, Cambridge University Press, v2.1.
Jake VanderPlas, *Python Data Science Handbook*, Shroff Publisher / O’Reilly Media.
Philipp Janert, *Data Analysis with Open Source Tools*, Shroff Publisher / O’Reilly Media.

Probabilistic separability based feature selection criteria

Secure execution environments and communications in cloud
